stories.title: Trump says U.S. military has rescued airman shot down over Iran
stories.description: A U.S. Airforce officer whose plane was shot down in Iran was rescued by U.S. forces early Sunday, President Trump announced on social media.

stories.title: U.S. searches for airman shot down in Iran, while Trump posts ultimatum
stories.description: At the start of the sixth week of war, U.S. forces search for a missing airman and President Trump reiterates his ultimatum for Iran to open the Straight of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran war enters its 6th week as military searches for downed jet crew member
stories.description: The war in Iran enters its 6th week as the search continues for the missing U.S. service member who bailed out of a fighter jet shot down over Iran on Friday.

stories.title: Trump's criticism of NATO raises questions of whether — or how — the U.S. could leave
stories.description: President Trump has complained that some NATO states aren't letting him use bases in the war against Iran. His criticism has left many wondering if the U.S. may try to withdraw from the alliance.

stories.title: What we know so far about rescue of US airman in Iran
stories.description: The US has rescued the missing crew member of the US F-15 fighter jet which was shot down over southern Iran.

stories.title: Search for missing airman presents serious test for US
stories.description: The stakes could rise further if the weapons system officer of the downed F15 Eagle plane is captured by Iran.

stories.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
stories.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

stories.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
stories.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

stories.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
stories.description: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: Iran's two largest steel plants shut down due to air strikes, companies say
stories.description: The strikes, which Iran says were first launched by Israel in co-ordination with the US last week, could cause major damage to the Iranian economy.

stories.title: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
stories.description: The comments were an apparent jab at the US leader's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran.

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffered suspected heart attack in prison, family says
stories.description: Narges Mohammadi's brother says he fears her life is in imminent danger and she needs to be transferred to a hospital.

stories.title: UK and allies discuss sanctions to stop Iran blocking Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Yvette Cooper said coordinated diplomatic and economic measures are required to reopen the shipping lane.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war objectives 'nearing completion' in address to nation
stories.description: The US president's first televised address of the conflict with Iran did little to quell uneasy markets.

stories.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
stories.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

stories.title: Pope Leo condemns ‘violence of war’ in first Easter Mass amid Iran conflict
stories.description: Pope Leo XIV celebrated his first Easter Mass as pontiff with a condemnation of “the violence of war that kills and destroys,’’ amid the US-Israeli war on Iran and Russia’s campaign in Ukraine. In his homily, the pontiff singled out those who wage war, abuse the weak and prioriti...

stories.title: US aviator missing after Iran shot down fighter jet has been rescued
stories.description: David Smith, Guardian's Washington bureau chief, is our guest.

stories.title: Ramstein: Controversy in Germany over military base's role in Middle East war
stories.description: Although Germany insists it is not a party to the war in Iran, the U.S. airbase in Ramstein plays a key role in coordinating American military operations causing controversy.

stories.title: US President Trump gives Iran 48 hours to open Strait of Hormuz, Iran rejects
stories.description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that Tehran had 48 hours left to cut a deal or face "all Hell", as US and Iranian forces scrambled to find a downed American airman.

stories.title: War in the Middle East: US rescues pilot from downed fighter jet
stories.description: Reza Sayah, our correspondent in Tehran, has more.

stories.title: In Iran, social media posts noted explosions, aircraft in area US jet was downed
stories.description: Reporting from Tehran, FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah says that around 3am local time, social media posts mentioned "explosions and aircraft near the city of Yasuj in central Iran" where the US F-15 jet was downed. Around an hour later, President Donald Trump said US forces had safely re...

stories.title: Crew member from downed US fighter jet rescued in 'miraculous' operation, says Trump
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said Sunday that US forces had safely recovered a second airman downed in Iran, calling it "miraculous" and "one of the most daring search and rescue operations in US History".

stories.title: Middle East war live: Iran says five killed in strikes during US pilot rescue
stories.description: Strikes killed five people overnight in Iran's southwest during the rescue of a missing US crewmember of a downed F-15 fighter jet, Iranian media reported Sunday. Iran also said that it destroyed three US aircrafts – two Black Hawk helicopters and one C-130 transport aircraft – d...

stories.title: Trump gives Iran 48 hours to make a deal as search continues for missing US pilot
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that Tehran had 48 hours left to cut a deal or face "all Hell", as US and Iranian forces scrambled to find a downed American airman.

stories.title: NATO anniversary overshadowed by Trump’s threats
stories.description: On this day, April 4th, 1949, NATO was founded, marking a pivotal moment in global security. Nearly 80 years later, however, the alliance's future is being questioned by its most powerful member, the United States. To understand the significance of NATO's formation, it’s worth re...

stories.title: 'Iran open to negotiations': Diplomacy shows signs of progress, 'damage to global economy continues'
stories.description: Gavin Lee is pleased to welcome Dr Christian Bueger is a Research Fellow at UNIDIR, and the Academy of International Affairs NRW, and Professor of International Relations at the University of Copenhagen. He offers an analysis of the evolving crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, emphas...

stories.title: Kuwait’s power, water plants damaged as Iran keeps attacking Gulf states
stories.description: Bahrain and the UAE also reported attacks resulting in fires, which were put out quickly.

stories.title: Thousands rally in Iraq against ‘senseless’ US-Israel war on Iran
stories.description: Protesters chant against the US and Israel, urging the global community to confront their actions in the Middle East.

stories.title: Trump seeks to end the war without it becoming ‘an abject failure’
stories.description: Barbara Slavin of the Stimson Center says Trump misread the situation in Iran and now scrambles for a way out.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 37 of US-Israeli attacks?
stories.description: A US crew member who has been missing since Iran shot down a fighter jet has been rescued, President Donald Trump says.

stories.title: Yemen’s Houthis claim cluster missile attack on Israel’s Ben Gurion airport
stories.description: Yemen’s Houthi military spokesman has claimed responsibility for a strike on Israel’s Ben Gurion airport in Jaffa.

stories.title: ‘Miraculous’: US rescues second pilot downed in Iran, Trump confirms
stories.description: Iran claims another US aircraft involved in the rescue of the F-15 fighter pilot has been shot down.

stories.title: Trump’s unpopular war on Iran
stories.description: Most Americans oppose the US-Israel war on Iran. ​​No recent US war has ever started off being this unpopular.

stories.title: Iran says Iraqi ships can pass Strait of Hormuz as transits tick up
stories.description: Tehran says Iraq will face no restrictions in waterway, praising country's 'struggle' against the US.

stories.title: US satellite firm Planet Labs announces blackout on war on Iran images
stories.description: Company says move amid US-Israel war on Iran comes after a request from the US government.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump confirms rescue of US officer, says ‘we got him’
stories.description: Iran says US-Israeli attacks on the Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone have killed five and wounded 170.

stories.title: How will Pakistan deal with the fallout from Iran war?
stories.description: War in the Middle East is worsening economic crisis in Pakistan.

stories.title: Israeli police arrest antiwar protesters in Tel Aviv
stories.description: Israeli police clashed with protesters and made arrests in Tel Aviv as demonstrators rallied against the war on Iran.

stories.title: Tehran psychiatric hospital not usable after US-Israeli strike
stories.description: A psychiatric hospital in Tehran is no longer functioning after a US-Israeli strike, the hospital chief says.

stories.title: Universities hit as US, Israel ramp up attacks on Iran’s infrastructure
stories.description: Authorities say at least 30 universities impacted by US and Israeli strikes since the start of the war.

stories.title: Trump confirms rescue of second crewmember of fighter jet in Iran
stories.description: According to the US president, the serviceman was injured

stories.title: Two power plants in Kuwait damaged in Iranian attack — energy ministry
stories.description: According to the ministry, desalination units have also sustained damage

stories.title: At least three people killed in missile attack in southwest Iran
stories.description: The governor of the Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad Province says that the exact number of dead and injured people can be significantly higher

stories.title: Explosions rock Isfahan in Central Iran — TV
stories.description: According to Al Hadath, the strikes are delivered by US and Israeli forces

stories.title: Five killed in attack on petrochemical complex in Iran’s Mahshahr
stories.description: According to the agency, at least five people were killed in an airstrike on the petrochemical complex

stories.title: Iraq exempted from restrictions on passage through Strait of Hormuz — Iranian military
stories.description: The headquarters stressed that "the restrictions apply only to hostile countries."

stories.title: Israeli army says it simultaneously struck over 200 targets in Iran and Lebanon
stories.description: In Lebanon, the strikes targeted military facilities of the Shiite organization Hezbollah

stories.title: Iran says it struck UAE aluminum industry facilities
stories.description: According to the statement, the United States and Israel "have significant investments in the UAE aluminum industry and use it to produce military equipment"

stories.title: Bushehr NPP continues operating without interruptions — authorities
stories.description: According to the statement, "the Bushehr nuclear power plant continues operating without any interruptions, and all processes are under full control and supervision."

stories.title: Main evacuation of Rosatom staff from Iran begins today — Rosatom CEO
stories.description: Likhachev added that the Iranian side is doing a great deal to ensure the safety of the Rosatom staff evacuation route, and cooperation with the Armenian government is proceeding smoothly

stories.title: Situation at Bushehr NPP is nearing a dangerous line — Zakharova
stories.description: According to the diplomat, Moscow has taken note of the deep concern expressed by IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi over the attack

stories.title: Russian Consulate General in Dubai has no reports of Russians injured in Iran strikes
stories.description: Earlier, the UAE Defense Ministry said in its daily report that a total of 217 people had been injured as a result of the strikes

stories.title: Slovak PM calls for lifting sanctions on Russian gas and oil imports
stories.description: Oil supplies to Slovakia and Hungary via the Druzhba pipeline through Ukraine were halted on January 27

stories.title: Trump says Iran has 48 hours to make deal or open strait
stories.description: Time is running out: 48 hours remain before all hell rains down on them, US President said

stories.title: Number of injured in Iran’s attacks on UAE rises to 217, including Russian citizens
stories.description: The previous day, 203 injured were reported, and there were no Russian citizens among them

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
